schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Minimal Model Equations: (ρ_rm Vac, P, ω, Φ_B, τ)

theory.canonical_statement: \subsection*{1. Minimal model equations} We group five observables $(\rho_{\rm vac}, p, \omega, \Phi_B, \tau)$ and write the minimal closed system: \begin{align} \dot{\rho}_{\rm vac} &=-\Gamma(\rho_{\rm vac})\,\Theta(p-p_c),\\ \dot{\omega} &= \alpha\,\mu_0\,B - \beta\,\omega^3,\\ \dot{\Phi}_B &= -\nabla\cdot \mathbf{E}_{\rm ind} + \eta\,\nabla^2 \Phi_B,\\ \dot{\tau} &= \kappa\,\omega\,\Phi_B - \lambda\,\tau, \end{align} with the TZP trigger at Eq.~\eqref{eq:TZP}. Parameters $\alpha,\beta,\eta,\kappa,\lambda$ are fit to observations.
theory.technical_interpretation: Minimal Model Equations: (ρ_rm Vac, P, ω, Φ_B, τ) is an expectation / trace over state, written (ρ_rm vac, p, ω, Φ_B, τ). It is computed as an expectation value or trace over a quantum state, producing an observable. It is built from ω, τ, v, a, c, p. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.inputs: ω, τ, v, a, c, p
formation.note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Minimal model equations’.
